findings.description: B2 level falls through to A1 graph in getGraphForLevel — silent substitution
findings.evidence: getGraphForLevel("B2") returns a1Graph via default case. B2 users see A1 content.
findings.fix: Add explicit B2 case that either returns a B2 graph or throws. Make parameter type CEFRLevel, not string. Add exhaustive switch check.

findings.description: productionGap field is never written — always empty object
findings.evidence: computeProductionGap exists but is never called by recordResult or any hook. productionGap stays as {} forever. Scheduler reads it but always gets 0.
findings.fix: Call computeProductionGap in recordResult after error logging, or remove the field and scheduler references to avoid false advertising.

findings.description: vocabularyMastery field declared but never read or written
findings.evidence: VocabularyClusterMastery type exists. createEmptyFingerprint initializes it as {}. No function in the engine touches it.
findings.fix: Remove the field or implement vocabulary tracking. Dead schema creates false expectations.

findings.evidence: aggregateErrorPatterns generates patterns but never sets rootCauseConceptId or rootCauseConfidence. Diagnosis engine runs separately but does not write back to patterns.
findings.fix: Either wire diagnosis output into error patterns or remove the fields.

findings.description: Three exercise types (dictation, reading-comprehension, free-writing) are declared but can never be scheduled
findings.evidence: ExerciseType union includes them. No scheduler pool (PRODUCTION_EXERCISES, RECOGNITION_EXERCISES, etc.) contains them. They are dead variants.
findings.fix: Remove from ExerciseType or add to appropriate scheduler pool.

findings.description: SessionRecipe.minNewVocabItems is declared (default 3) but never enforced
findings.evidence: DEFAULT_SESSION_RECIPE sets minNewVocabItems: 3. generateSession never reads this field.
findings.fix: Enforce in scheduler or remove to avoid false documentation.

findings.description: Three different graduation/mastery criteria exist — inconsistent gates
findings.evidence: isMastered: rawScore+confidence+attempts+days. isGraduated: rawScore+attempts (no confidence). buildWeeklyCheckItems grad filter: endScore only (no attempts). A concept can graduate weekly sprint without meeting isMastered.
findings.fix: Unify to a single mastery gate, or explicitly document why each gate differs.

findings.description: Level progression only implemented for A1→A2 — no A2→B1 or B1→B2
findings.evidence: checkA1Complete in useFingerprint.ts triggers A2 promotion. No equivalent for A2→B1 exists.
findings.fix: Implement checkA2Complete and checkB1Complete, or document that level-up is manual only after A2.

tests.name: A: Fingerprint Contract A1: Structure integrity createEmptyFingerprint returns all required fields
A: Fingerprint Contract A1: Structure integrity currentLevel is always a valid CEFRLevel
A: Fingerprint Contract A1: Structure integrity passedSentenceIds is a record, not an array
A: Fingerprint Contract A2: Seeding and initial mastery seedInitialMastery populates root concepts for A1 graph
A: Fingerprint Contract A2: Seeding and initial mastery seedInitialMastery does NOT seed dependent concepts
A: Fingerprint Contract A2: Seeding and initial mastery seedInitialMastery is idempotent — second call is a no-op
A: Fingerprint Contract A2: Seeding and initial mastery each level graph has at least 5 concepts
A: Fingerprint Contract A2: Seeding and initial mastery concept IDs are unique within each graph
A: Fingerprint Contract A3: Mastery scoring correctness updateConceptMastery increases rawScore on correct answer
A: Fingerprint Contract A3: Mastery scoring correctness updateConceptMastery decreases rawScore on wrong answer
A: Fingerprint Contract A3: Mastery scoring correctness slip detection reduces impact of wrong answer after strong streak
A: Fingerprint Contract A3: Mastery scoring correctness SRS level advances on correct and resets on wrong
A: Fingerprint Contract A3: Mastery scoring correctness nextReviewAt is always set after an answer
A: Fingerprint Contract A3: Mastery scoring correctness isMastered requires ALL four gates
A: Fingerprint Contract A4: Decay system refreshDecay reduces decayedScore for stale concepts
A: Fingerprint Contract A4: Decay system decay floor is 35, never zero
A: Fingerprint Contract A4: Decay system recently practiced concepts do not decay significantly
A: Fingerprint Contract A5: Weak / decaying / review-due concept detection getPrimaryWeakConcepts returns weakest by decayedScore
A: Fingerprint Contract A5: Weak / decaying / review-due concept detection getDecayingConcepts detects rawScore >= 70 but decayedScore < 70
A: Fingerprint Contract A5: Weak / decaying / review-due concept detection getReviewDueConcepts finds SRS-due concepts
A: Fingerprint Contract A6: Phase model locked when prerequisites not met
A: Fingerprint Contract A6: Phase model intro when fewer than 5 attempts
A: Fingerprint Contract A6: Phase model practice when >= 5 attempts and score < 70
A: Fingerprint Contract A6: Phase model consolidation when score 70-84
A: Fingerprint Contract A6: Phase model maintenance when score >= 85
A: Fingerprint Contract A7: Fingerprint feeds the scheduler scheduler reads fingerprint.conceptMastery to build weak concepts
A: Fingerprint Contract A7: Fingerprint feeds the scheduler scheduler reads fingerprint.passedSentenceIds to filter content
A: Fingerprint Contract A7: Fingerprint feeds the scheduler scheduler reads fingerprint.weeklyFocus for remediation bias
A: Fingerprint Contract A7: Fingerprint feeds the scheduler scheduler reads fingerprint.currentLevel via session output
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B1: Session is fingerprint-driven, not random different fingerprints produce different sessions
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B1: Session is fingerprint-driven, not random empty fingerprint produces cold-start session with new-material items
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B1: Session is fingerprint-driven, not random fingerprint with weak concepts produces remediation-heavy session
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B2: Passed-sentence suppression remediation excludes passed sentences — concept with all passed gets skipped
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B2: Passed-sentence suppression new-material excludes passed sentences
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B2: Passed-sentence suppression review ALLOWS passed sentences — SRS spaced repetition
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B2: Passed-sentence suppression session does not collapse when many sentences are passed
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B3: Level-specific behavior A1 and A2 sessions use different concept pools
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B3: Level-specific behavior A1 and B1 sessions use entirely different concept pools
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B3: Level-specific behavior session.level matches fingerprint.currentLevel
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B4: Recipe ratios session has approximately the right number of items (~16-17 at default)
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B4: Recipe ratios calibration recipe overrides default when calibrationSessionsRemaining > 0
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B5: SelectionReason integrity every session item has a valid selectionReason
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B5: SelectionReason integrity remediation items have weak_concept or weekly_focus reason
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B5: SelectionReason integrity review items have review_due, decaying, or weak_concept reason
B: Scheduler / Daily Oppgaver B6: Production guarantee session contains at least one production exercise type
C: Weekly Progress Contract C1: Weekly focus is fingerprint-driven selectWeeklyFocus returns concepts from fingerprint mastery, not hardcoded
C: Weekly Progress Contract C1: Weekly focus is fingerprint-driven different fingerprints produce different focus sets
C: Weekly Progress Contract C1: Weekly focus is fingerprint-driven SRS-due concepts are included in weekly focus
C: Weekly Progress Contract C2: Week open/close lifecycle openWeek sets weeklyFocus, weekStartedAt, and weekStartSnapshots
C: Weekly Progress Contract C2: Week open/close lifecycle openWeek is idempotent — second call on open week is a no-op
C: Weekly Progress Contract C2: Week open/close lifecycle closeWeek records real focus outcomes from fingerprint delta
C: Weekly Progress Contract C2: Week open/close lifecycle closeWeek without activity still records zero-attempt outcomes
C: Weekly Progress Contract C3: Week reset and abandonment shouldResetWeek returns true after 7 days
C: Weekly Progress Contract C3: Week reset and abandonment shouldResetWeek returns false within 7 days
C: Weekly Progress Contract C3: Week reset and abandonment ensureWeekOpen closes stale week as abandoned and opens new one
C: Weekly Progress Contract C3: Week reset and abandonment ensureWeekOpen is a no-op when week is current
C: Weekly Progress Contract C4: Weekly check items are fingerprint-driven buildWeeklyCheckItems produces items for focus concepts
C: Weekly Progress Contract C4: Weekly check items are fingerprint-driven weekly check items have purpose=review and selectionReason=review_due
C: Weekly Progress Contract C5: Daily-to-weekly sync integrity practice during a week changes mastery that closeWeek captures
C: Weekly Progress Contract C5: Daily-to-weekly sync integrity weekly history cap is 26 entries
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D1: Single-session proof correct answer → mastery increases → sentence marked passed → excluded from next session
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D1: Single-session proof wrong answer → error logged → mastery decreases → concept stays in remediation
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D2: Multi-session progressive mastery 5 sessions of correct answers → mastery rises → concept leaves weak pool
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D3: Weekly sprint lifecycle proof full weekly cycle: open → practice → close → new week with updated focus
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D4: Passed-sentence suppression across sessions sentences passed in session 1 are excluded from session 2 remediation
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D5: Level transition proof A1 fingerprint generates A1 session with A1 concepts
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D5: Level transition proof A2 fingerprint generates A2 session with A2 concepts
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D6: Error accumulation drives diagnosis repeated errors on same tag accumulate in recentErrors and form patterns
D: End-to-End Proof — Full Adaptive Chain D7: Content-loader level isolation (CRITICAL) getGraphForLevel returns correct graph for each supported level
